Title: PIF4-dependent 2-hydroxymelatonin and 3-hydroxymelatonin biosynthesis is involved in skotomorphogenic seedling growth in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase family (inferred from known plant M2H assignment)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream conversion of melatonin to hydroxylated melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates melatonin at the 2-position to form 2-hydroxymelatonin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes skotomorphogenic seedling growth and hypocotyl elongation; induces gibberellin and brassinosteroid biosynthesis-related gene expression during seed imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; induced at night in seedlings and mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of M2H contributes to reduced 2-OHM accumulation and shorter hypocotyls; overexpression promotes hypocotyl elongation and elevates GA/BR biosynthetic gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Nocturnal peak; higher at night in 6-day-old seedlings and 4-week-old plants; repressed in bri1, bak1, and pif4pif5; elevated in phyB
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nocturnal peak; higher at night in 6-day-old seedlings and 4-week-old plants; repressed in bri1, bak1, and pif4pif5; elevated in phyB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native M2H promoter; M2H promoter::GUS reporter in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Darkness; 14-h light/10-h dark cycle; seed imbibition at 23°C in darkness; cold imbibition at 4°C in darkness; exogenous 2-OHM+3-OHM treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: m2h contributes to dwarf/short hypocotyl phenotype in combination with m3h; no major additional tradeoff specifically assigned to single m2h in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro recombinant enzyme assay with kinetics; subcellular localization; T-DNA knockout and double mutant phenotyping; overexpression; qRT-PCR; hydroxylated melatonin quantification; transient promoter activation assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate metabolic engineering gene to elevate hydroxylated melatonin and enhance early seedling elongation/growth under dark conditions, potentially via GA/BR induction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtM2H (AT3g60290) was cloned and biochemically validated as Arabidopsis melatonin 2-hydroxylase. 2. M2H localizes to chloroplasts and shows night-peaked expression dependent on PIF4/5 and BR signaling. 3. M2H loss, especially with M3H loss, reduces hydroxylated melatonin levels and seedling growth, while overexpression promotes hypocotyl elongation and induces GA/BR biosynthetic genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis M2H was identified as a rice M2H homolog with 43% amino acid identity to rice M2H (AK119413). Double mutant m2hm3h had lower 2-OHM and 3-OHM after melatonin feeding, suggesting functional coupling with M3H and possible family redundancy.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase family (inferred from known plant M3H assignment)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream conversion of melatonin to hydroxylated melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates melatonin at the 3-position to form 3-hydroxymelatonin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes skotomorphogenic seedling growth and hypocotyl elongation; induces gibberellin and brassinosteroid biosynthesis-related gene expression during seed imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; induced at night in seedlings and mature plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of M3H contributes to shorter hypocotyls and, together with M2H loss, causes dwarfism; overexpression promotes hypocotyl elongation and elevates GA/BR biosynthetic gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Nocturnal peak; repressed in bri1, bak1, and pif4pif5; elevated in phyB
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nocturnal peak; repressed in bri1, bak1, and pif4pif5; elevated in phyB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native M3H promoter; M3H promoter::GUS reporter in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Darkness; 14-h light/10-h dark cycle; seed imbibition at 23°C in darkness; cold imbibition at 4°C in darkness; exogenous 2-OHM+3-OHM treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: m3h contributes to reduced growth when mutated; double mutation with m2h causes stronger dwarf phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic knockout and double mutant phenotyping; overexpression; qRT-PCR; transient promoter activation assay; prior gene identity integrated with this study’s functional genetics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate engineering gene to increase hydroxylated melatonin and stimulate early growth via GA/BR biosynthesis during seed imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. M3H acts with M2H as a core hydroxylated melatonin biosynthetic enzyme promoting seedling growth. 2. M3H expression peaks at night and depends on PIF4/5 and BR signaling while being repressed by PhyB. 3. M3H overexpression promotes hypocotyl elongation and increases GA/BR biosynthetic gene expression, whereas loss contributes to dwarf/short hypocotyl phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses previously characterized Arabidopsis M3H and demonstrates its joint function with M2H in the double mutant m2hm3h. Discussion states M3H is cytoplasmic.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; phytochrome-interacting factor family
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding; integrates light, BR, and GA-associated signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates M2H and M3H transcription by binding E-box motifs in their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Hydroxylated melatonin biosynthesis linked to GA and BR biosynthetic activation during seed imbibition and skotomorphogenic growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIF4 positively controls 2-OHM/3-OHM biosynthesis and seedling growth; reduced PIF4 in m2hm3h is associated with shorter hypocotyl and reduced growth-related gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PIF4 transcript and protein accumulate during day/night cycle in WT but are reduced in m2hm3h; functional in dark growth
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PIF4 transcript and protein accumulate during day/night cycle in WT but are reduced in m2hm3h; functional in dark growth
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Darkness; light/dark cycle; phyB background; seed imbibition; β-estradiol-induced transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: PhyB-mediated light signaling; brassinosteroid signaling via BRI1/BAK1 and likely BZR1; GA-DELLA status; COP1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential direct activator of M2H/M3H expression and thus a key upstream regulator of hydroxylated melatonin accumulation in skotomorphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely functions with BZR1 as PIF4-BZR1 heterodimer (proposed, not directly proven here)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Hydroxylated melatonin deficiency in m2hm3h lowers PIF4 transcript/protein, suggesting positive feedback/feedforward reinforcement between PIF4 and 2-OHM/3-OHM-associated growth signaling
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PIF5; PhyB; BZR1 (proposed); DELLA/RGL2; COP1-associated regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pif4pif5 mutant expression analysis; EMSA showing binding to M2H/M3H promoter E-boxes; transient transactivation in Nicotiana; immunoblotting of PIF4 protein; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pif4pif5 exhibits dwarfism, short hypocotyl, reduced leaf area, reduced petiole length, and lower biomass
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: M2H promoter; M3H promoter; XVE-inducible PIF4 construct in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream regulatory target for engineering hydroxylated melatonin-mediated seedling vigor, but manipulation may broadly alter light/hormone growth programs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF4 directly binds E-box motifs in M2H and M3H promoters and activates their transcription. 2. M2H/M3H expression is reduced in pif4pif5 and elevated in phyB, placing PIF4 downstream of light signaling and upstream of hydroxylated melatonin biosynthesis. 3. Reduced PIF4 transcript and protein in m2hm3h indicates reciprocal reinforcement between hydroxylated melatonin production and the skotomorphogenic growth module.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transient activation was tested in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; the metabolite effect was investigated in Arabidopsis, so source and applied species remain Arabidopsis for PIF4 while the assay host differs.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; phytochrome-interacting factor family
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulator required with PIF4 for full nighttime expression of M2H and M3H
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Hydroxylated melatonin biosynthesis linked to skotomorphogenic growth
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote skotomorphogenic seedling growth and induce GA/BR biosynthesis-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with PIF4 to promote M2H/M3H expression and normal hypocotyl elongation in darkness
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inferred from pif4pif5 mutant phenotype; specific expression pattern not detailed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred from pif4pif5 mutant phenotype; specific expression pattern not detailed
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important co-regulator with PIF4 for nighttime induction of hydroxylated melatonin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with PIF4 in shared regulatory module; direct complex not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pif4pif5 double mutant expression and phenotype analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss with PIF4 causes dwarfism and reduced hypocotyl elongation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target with PIF4 for manipulating hydroxylated melatonin-dependent dark growth, with caution due to broad developmental roles
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF5 is required together with PIF4 for normal nighttime expression of M2H and M3H. 2. The pif4pif5 mutant phenocopies m2hm3h in multiple growth traits, supporting upstream control of hydroxylated melatonin biosynthesis. 3. PIF5 is part of the PhyB-PIF4/5-M2H/M3H skotomorphogenic signaling module.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding to M2H/M3H promoters was only demonstrated for PIF4, not PIF5, in this paper.
